title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 9 January 2025.

items.title: **McDonalds** launches **Sonic 3** themed Happy Meals in the UK today
bullets: McDonalds Japan is currently running an extensive Evangelion campaign

items.title: Minecraft YouTuber to present new **Classic FM** video game music series
bullets: Next Level with DanTDM launches on Saturday 11th January at 9pm for an hour, and will run each week until Easter.
The first episode will focus on legendary soundtracks from games such as The Legend of Zelda, Final Fantasy 7, The Elder Scrolls, and of course Minecraft.

items.title: **Final Fantasy 7** director would ‘love’ another movie based on the game
**Squid Game: Unleashed** reaches over 10m installs
bullets: Battle royale multiplayer has become Netflix's fifth most downloaded game
For launch, is temporarily free to all (even those without a Netflix account), though this is only the case for a limited period
Most of Squid Game: Unleashed's downloads are from Apple's App Store at 7.4 million installs, with 3.3 million from Google Play.
The United States was the top country for installs at 1.8 million, followed by Brazil with 1.1 million downloads.

items.title: **Plaion** CEO Klemens Kundratitz steps down 31 years after co-founding Koch Media
bullets: Deputy CEO Phil Rogers will assume leadership

items.title: **Splash Damage** confirms layoff plans as Transformers game is cancelled
bullets: The London-based studio behind Gears Tactics, Wolfenstein: Enemy Territory and Dirty Bomb announced the news on Wednesday in an X post.
Unclear which party decided to end development on Transformers: Reactive
Undisclosed amount of people now "at risk of redundancy"
